title: arum orientale subsp. orientale
intro: Arum orientale subsp. orientale, also known as the Wild Cat's Eye or Wild Iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Arum. It is a perennial plant with a bulbous rhizome, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, sore throat, and respiratory problems. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tincture, decoction, or ointment, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
